source.notes: Raw Data row 1 contains headers. Metadata/sample-rate rows are skipped before processing frame data.

uncertain.note: Human confirmation needed for sign convention and preferred event window.
Workbook does not label load, foot plant, or contact events, so this uses the full processed capture.
Uses peak dominant-wrist speed as an impact proxy because the workbook has no contact event marker.
Left/right sign direction requires human confirmation.
Dominant-side shoulder is used as the rear-shoulder proxy and needs lab confirmation.
Assumes CenterOfGravity/X is in meters and converts displacement to centimeters.

impactProxy.method: Peak smoothed dominant-wrist speed within central 80% of capture

metrics.name: Shoulder-Hip Separation
metrics.finding: Peak trunk separation in the workbook is 70.2° at 85% of the capture.
metrics.whyItMatters: Separation describes how the pelvis and upper trunk are organizing rotational stretch before delivery.
metrics.trainingFocus: Use as a measured reference point after confirming the lab's positive/negative convention.
metrics.reviewNote: Human confirmation needed for sign convention and preferred event window.

metrics.name: Torso Rotation
metrics.finding: Shoulder twist range across the processed capture is 227.8°.
metrics.whyItMatters: Torso rotation range is a practical proxy for the rotational movement available to transfer into the barrel.
metrics.trainingFocus: Review against the intended capture window before using it as a normative grade.
metrics.reviewNote: Workbook does not label load, foot plant, or contact events, so this uses the full processed capture.

metrics.name: Lead Hip Internal Rotation
metrics.finding: Lead hip internal/external rotation is 0.0° at the wrist-speed impact proxy.
metrics.whyItMatters: Lead-hip rotation helps indicate how the front side is accepting and redirecting force.
metrics.trainingFocus: Confirm axis orientation before assigning a performance grade.
metrics.reviewNote: Uses peak dominant-wrist speed as an impact proxy because the workbook has no contact event marker.

metrics.name: Torso Lateral Flexion
metrics.finding: Trunk left/right tilt is -21.0° at the wrist-speed impact proxy.
metrics.whyItMatters: Lateral trunk position helps describe posture through the delivery window.
metrics.trainingFocus: Review with video/contact timing before converting this to a coaching conclusion.
metrics.reviewNote: Left/right sign direction requires human confirmation.

metrics.name: Rear Shoulder Loading
metrics.finding: Peak dominant-shoulder horizontal angle before the impact proxy is 54.0°.
metrics.whyItMatters: Rear-shoulder position helps describe how the upper body stores and releases into the swing.
metrics.trainingFocus: Confirm whether dominant shoulder maps to rear shoulder for this capture setup.
metrics.reviewNote: Dominant-side shoulder is used as the rear-shoulder proxy and needs lab confirmation.

metrics.name: Center of Gravity
metrics.finding: Center of gravity X changes -15.9 cm from first frame to the wrist-speed impact proxy.
metrics.whyItMatters: CoG drift helps describe how the athlete moves mass into the hitting window.
metrics.trainingFocus: Confirm global X direction and units before interpreting forward/backward drift.
metrics.reviewNote: Assumes CenterOfGravity/X is in meters and converts displacement to centimeters.

reportGraphs.title: Torso Extension During Rotation And Blocking Phase
Right Shoulder Horizontal Abduction During Loading Phase
